benchmark_metadata.purpose: Expanded H&N benchmark — sinonasal, oral subsites, salivary, skull base, rare histologies

cases.why_this_case_matters: Sinonasal SCC with orbital invasion — tests infrastructure maxillectomy vs orbital exenteration decision, skull base surgery, and limited sinonasal-specific trial data.

ITAC is an occupational cancer with distinct biology from sinonasal SCC. Tests endoscopic vs open craniofacial resection, proton therapy for skull base, and the unique association with wood dust exposure.

cases.stage: Kadish C (Hyams grade III, intracranial extension)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Rare neuroendocrine skull base tumor with unique Kadish staging. Tests craniofacial resection, proton/particle therapy, and platinum-etoposide chemo adapted from neuroendocrine paradigms.
SNUC is ultra-aggressive. IDH2 mutation defines a potentially targetable subtype. Tests neoadjuvant chemo, aggressive multimodal therapy, and IDH2 inhibitor (enasidenib) off-label.

cases.cancer_type: NUT carcinoma (NUT midline carcinoma of sinonasal tract)
cases.why_this_case_matters: NUT carcinoma is one of the most aggressive known human cancers (median OS ~6 months). BRD4-NUT fusion is pathognomonic. Tests BET inhibitors (birabresib, PLX51107), and the role of aggressive surgery despite poor prognosis.

Sinonasal mucosal melanoma — even rarer and worse prognosis than oral mucosal melanoma. KIT exon 11 MUTATION (not just amplification) predicts imatinib response. Tests KIT-targeted therapy, dual checkpoint, and endoscopic vs open resection.

cases.cancer_type: Floor of mouth squamous cell carcinoma
cases.why_this_case_matters: Floor of mouth SCC with mandible periosteal invasion — marginal vs segmental mandibulectomy decision. Tests surgical margins in a anatomically constrained site, reconstructive planning, and heavy smoking/alcohol comorbidity management.

Buccal SCC is common in South/Southeast Asia (betel quid). Oral submucous fibrosis is premalignant. Tests through-and-through resection with cheek reconstruction, trismus management, and betel cessation.

Hard palate SCC requires palatal resection and obturator prosthesis. Tests speech/swallowing rehabilitation, bone resection decisions, and the distinction between hard palate SCC and minor salivary gland tumors of the palate.

cases.cancer_type: Lip squamous cell carcinoma — locally advanced
cases.why_this_case_matters: Lip SCC has UV + tobacco dual etiology. Commissure involvement complicates reconstruction (Karapandzic/Abbe flap). Tests microvascular lip reconstruction, sentinel node biopsy for lip, and commissure-specific surgical planning.

cases.cancer_type: Parotid acinic cell carcinoma — high-grade transformation

risk_factors: prior low-grade parotid AcCC excised 8 years ago

cases.why_this_case_matters: High-grade transformation of acinic cell carcinoma — aggressive biology from originally indolent tumor. Facial nerve sacrifice decision. Tests NR4A3 as diagnostic marker, re-excision in previously operated field, and limited systemic options for AcCC.

cases.stage: Stage III (T3N0M0, perineural invasion along lingual nerve)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Submandibular ACC with perineural spread along lingual nerve — tests nerve tracing at surgery, adjuvant RT field design for perineural pathway, and long-term surveillance (ACC recurs up to 20+ years later).

cases.cancer_type: Merkel cell carcinoma of the head and neck
cases.why_this_case_matters: MCC is a neuroendocrine skin cancer with high immunotherapy response. MCPyV+ predicts avelumab/pembrolizumab benefit. But CLL + ibrutinib complicates immunotherapy. Tests the dual-malignancy treatment coordination challenge.

cases.stage: Locally advanced (extending to brainstem, encasing basilar artery)
cases.why_this_case_matters: Chordoma is a rare notochordal tumor requiring proton/carbon ion therapy. Tests skull base surgery (endoscopic endonasal), particle therapy as standard, brachyury-targeted approaches, and the challenge of surgically inaccessible tumors.

cases.cancer_type: Juvenile nasopharyngeal angiofibroma (JNA) — atypical late presentation

risk_factors: diagnosed at age 16, recurred multiple times

cases.why_this_case_matters: JNA is benign but locally destructive. Recurrent JNA at age 45 after multiple prior surgeries. Tests embolization + surgery, anti-androgen therapy (flutamide), bevacizumab for VEGF-driven growth, and radiation for multiply recurrent disease.

cases.cancer_type: External auditory canal squamous cell carcinoma
cases.stage: Stage III (T3N0M0, middle ear involvement, modified Pittsburgh staging)
cases.why_this_case_matters: EAC SCC is extremely rare (~1/million). Tests lateral temporal bone resection, adjuvant RT for skull base, and extremely limited evidence base requiring extrapolation from other H&N SCC data.

Retromolar trigone SCC is at the junction of oral cavity and oropharynx — treatment approach depends on extent. Pterygoid invasion makes surgery complex. Tests infratemporal fossa approach, composite resection with mandible, and trismus management.

Laryngeal neuroendocrine carcinoma — extremely rare, treated like SCLC or LCNEC. Tests platinum-etoposide vs SCC approach debate, tarlatamab for DLL3+, and organ preservation feasibility in a neuroendocrine histology.

risk_factors: prior radiation to jaw region as child (retinoblastoma treatment)

cases.why_this_case_matters: Jaw osteosarcoma differs from extremity osteosarcoma — different chemo response rates, reconstructive challenges (free fibula flap). Prior radiation is a risk factor. Tests neoadjuvant MAP chemo, mandible reconstruction, and second radiation-induced malignancy considerations.

Extramedullary plasmacytoma is a plasma cell neoplasm distinct from myeloma. RT alone is curative in >90%. Tests whether strategy correctly avoids myeloma-intensity chemo, emphasizes RT 40-50 Gy, and includes long-term myeloma conversion surveillance.
